description: Run Amazon Q in your workspace to access Amazon's AI coding assistant with MCP integration and task reporting.

variables.name: agent_config
variables.description: Optional Agent configuration JSON for Amazon Q.

variables.name: agent_id
variables.description: The ID of a Coder agent.

variables.name: agentapi_chat_based_path
variables.description: Use chat-based path for AgentAPI.

variables.name: agentapi_version
variables.description: The version of AgentAPI to install.

variables.name: ai_prompt
variables.description: The initial task prompt to send to Amazon Q.

variables.name: amazon_q_version
variables.description: The version of Amazon Q to install.

variables.name: auth_tarball
variables.description: Base64 encoded, zstd compressed tarball of a pre-authenticated ~/.local/share/amazon-q directory.

variables.name: cli_app
variables.description: Whether to create a CLI app for Amazon Q

variables.name: cli_app_display_name
variables.description: Display name for the CLI app

variables.name: coder_mcp_instructions
variables.description: Instructions for the Coder MCP server integration.
variables.default:
YOU MUST REPORT ALL TASKS TO CODER.
When reporting tasks you MUST follow these EXACT instructions:
- IMMEDIATELY report status after receiving ANY user message
- Be granular If you are investigating with multiple steps report each step to coder.

Task state MUST be one of the following:
- Use "state": "working" when actively processing WITHOUT needing additional user input
- Use "state": "complete" only when finished with a task
- Use "state": "failure" when you need ANY user input lack sufficient details or encounter blockers.

Task summaries MUST:
- Include specifics about what you're doing
- Include clear and actionable steps for the user
- Be less than 160 characters in length

variables.name: icon
variables.description: The icon to use for the app.

variables.name: install_agentapi
variables.description: Whether to install AgentAPI.

variables.name: install_amazon_q
variables.description: Whether to install Amazon Q.

variables.name: post_install_script
variables.description: Optional script to run after installing Amazon Q.

variables.name: pre_install_script
variables.description: Optional script to run before installing Amazon Q.

variables.name: q_install_url
variables.description: Base URL for Amazon Q installation downloads.

variables.name: report_tasks
variables.description: Whether to enable task reporting to Coder UI via AgentAPI

variables.name: system_prompt
variables.description: The system prompt to use for Amazon Q. This should instruct the agent how to do task reporting.
variables.default:
You are a helpful Coding assistant. Aim to autonomously investigate
and solve issues the user gives you and test your work, whenever possible.
Avoid shortcuts like mocking tests. When you get stuck, you can ask the user
but opt for autonomy.

variables.name: trust_all_tools
variables.description: Whether to trust all tools in Amazon Q.

variables.name: web_app_display_name
variables.description: Display name for the web app

variables.name: workdir
variables.description: The folder to run Amazon Q in.
